about.meta_description: Learn about {{name}} — a time banking platform where communities exchange skills using time as currency.
about.hero_description: {{name}} is a modern time banking platform where every hour of service is valued equally. We help communities exchange skills, build trust, and create a fairer local economy — one hour at a time.

values.title: Our Values
values.subtitle: The principles that guide everything we do at {{name}}.

0.title: Equality
0.description: Every person's time is valued equally. One hour of gardening is worth the same as one hour of tutoring.

1.title: Community
1.description: We believe in building strong local connections. Every exchange strengthens the fabric of your neighbourhood.

2.title: Trust & Safety
2.description: Reviews, ratings, and broker oversight ensure a safe environment for all members to participate.

3.title: Sustainability
3.description: By sharing skills locally, we reduce waste, support circular economies, and strengthen local resilience.

cta.title: Ready to Get Involved?

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.subtitle: Have a question about {{name}}? We'd love to hear from you.
contact.success_message: We'll get back to you as soon as possible.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle_before_link: Can't find what you're looking for? Visit our
faq.no_results: No matching questions found. Try a different search term.
faq.cta_description: Our support team is here to help.

q1.question: What is time banking?
q1.answer_p1: Time banking is a community exchange system where one hour of help equals one time credit, regardless of the service provided. Whether you're teaching a language, helping with gardening, or providing tech support, your time is valued equally.
q1.answer_p2: It's a way to build community connections while exchanging skills and services without traditional money.

q2.question: Is it free to join?
q2.answer_p1: Yes! Membership is completely free. The only currency used is time credits, which you earn by helping others in the community.
q2.answer_p2: New members often receive a small number of starter credits to help them get started.

q3.question: How do I get started?
q3.step1_text: Add a photo, bio, and your skills
q3.step2_text: See what services are offered and needed
q3.step3_text: Share what you can help others with

q4.question: What skills can I offer?
q4.answer_outro: Everyone has something valuable to offer!

q1.question: What is a time credit?
q1.answer_p1: One time credit equals one hour of service. It's the currency of our community.
q1.answer_p2: You can also exchange partial hours: 30 minutes = 0.5 credits, 15 minutes = 0.25 credits.

q2.question: How do I earn time credits?

q3.question: Can I donate my credits?
q3.option1: Transfer credits to any other member
q3.option2: Donate to the community pot (helps members who need extra support)

q4.question: Can my balance go negative?
q4.answer: Some communities allow limited negative balances to help new members get started before they've had a chance to earn credits. Check with your community coordinator for the specific rules in your timebank.

q5.question: What if I sent credits to the wrong person?
q5.answer_intro: Transactions cannot be automatically reversed. If you made a mistake:
q5.step1: Contact the recipient and ask them to send the credits back
q5.step2: If you can't resolve it, contact support for assistance
q5.answer_outro: Always double-check the recipient before confirming a transfer.

q1.question: How do I arrange an exchange?
q1.step1: Find an offer or request that interests you
q1.step2: Message the member to discuss details
q1.step3: Agree on when, where, and estimated time
q1.step5: The person who received help sends time credits
q1.step6: Leave a review for each other

q2.question: Is it safe to meet strangers?
q2.tip1: Meet in public places for first exchanges
q2.tip2: Check the member's profile and reviews
q2.tip4: Trust your instincts — if something feels wrong, leave
q2.answer_outro: Report any concerning behaviour to our support team.

q3.question: What if someone doesn't show up or complete the service?
q3.answer_intro: Communication is key. If there's an issue:
q3.step1: Message the member to discuss what happened
q3.step2: Try to find a resolution together
q3.step3: If you can't resolve it, contact support
q3.answer_outro: Repeated no-shows or poor behaviour may result in account restrictions.

q4.question: Are the services insured?
q4.answer: Exchanges are informal arrangements between community members. We don't provide insurance coverage for services. For higher-risk activities, discuss liability with the other member beforehand. Some members have their own professional insurance.

q1.question: What are badges and XP?
q1.answer_intro: Our gamification system rewards community participation:
q1.item1_text: Earned for various activities, helping you level up
q1.item2_text: Achievements for reaching milestones (60+ to collect!)
q1.item3_text: Progress from Newcomer to Timebank Legend

q2.question: How do I earn XP?
q2.answer_intro: You earn XP for many activities:

q3.question: Can I opt out of leaderboards?
q3.answer_before_link: Yes! If you prefer privacy, you can opt out of appearing on public leaderboards in your
q3.answer_after_link: . You'll still earn XP and badges, they just won't be visible to others on the rankings.

q1.question: How do I change my password?
q1.answer_after_link: > Security to change your password. You'll need to enter your current password first. If you've forgotten your password, use the "Forgot Password" link on the login page.

q2.question: Who can see my profile?
q2.answer_intro: By default, your profile is visible to other community members. You can control:

q3.question: How do I delete my account?
q3.answer_intro: If you wish to leave the community:
q3.answer_outro: Note: Your transaction history will be retained for record-keeping, but your personal information will be removed.

q4.question: How is my data protected?
q4.item1: Your data is encrypted and securely stored
q4.item3: You can request a copy of your data at any time
q4.item4: We comply with GDPR and data protection regulations

maintenance.title: We'll be back soon!
maintenance.description: is currently undergoing scheduled maintenance to improve your experience.
maintenance.apology: We apologize for any inconvenience. Please check back in a little while.

where_we_are.title: Where we are now
where_we_are.paragraph1: Project NEXUS is currently in Release Candidate (RC) — the final stage before declaring General Availability. The platform is feature-complete for V1 and is running live across multiple active communities worldwide. Our focus is now on stability hardening, performance tuning, and resolving edge-case issues before the full public launch.
where_we_are.paragraph2: Everything you see here runs on production infrastructure (Azure, Cloudflare, MariaDB, Redis). Your data is real and treated with the same care as any live system. This is not a demo or sandbox environment.

whats_stable.title: What's stable and working
whats_stable.core_item1: Member registration, login, two-factor auth (TOTP + WebAuthn), guided onboarding
whats_stable.core_item2: Time credit wallet — earning, spending, transfers, broker controls, and full transaction history
whats_stable.core_item3: Multi-tenant architecture — unlimited communities, each with isolated data and custom branding
whats_stable.core_item4: Exchange workflow — structured service exchange lifecycle with broker approval
whats_stable.core_item5: Federation — cross-community exchange, shared listings, events, and messaging across connected platforms
whats_stable.core_item6: Progressive Web App — installable on any device, works offline
whats_stable.member_item1: Service marketplace — post offers and requests, browse and smart-match listings
whats_stable.member_item2: Social feed — posts, comments, likes, polls, hashtags, and voice messages
whats_stable.member_item3: Messaging — direct conversations with read receipts and real-time updates
whats_stable.member_item4: Events — creating, discovering, RSVPs, and calendar export
whats_stable.member_item5: Groups — community sub-groups with discussions, roles, and moderation
whats_stable.member_item6: Group exchanges — bulk community service exchanges
whats_stable.member_item7: Members directory — browse, filter, and discover people
whats_stable.member_item8: Connections — follow and connect with community members
whats_stable.member_item9: Reviews and ratings — build trust through member feedback
whats_stable.member_item10: Endorsements — peer skill and experience endorsements
whats_stable.member_item12: Skills browse — explore skill taxonomy and discover expertise
whats_stable.member_item13: Volunteering — opportunities, shifts, applications, and hours logging
whats_stable.member_item14: Job vacancies — full recruitment module with alerts and analytics
whats_stable.member_item15: Organisations — company and employer profiles with sub-accounts
whats_stable.member_item16: Goals and impact — personal goals, mentoring, and community impact tracking
whats_stable.member_item17: Ideation challenges — innovation hub with campaigns, ideas, voting, and outcomes
whats_stable.member_item18: Achievements and gamification — XP, badges, challenges, streaks, leaderboard, and Nexus Score
whats_stable.member_item19: Personal insights — individual engagement metrics and personalised recommendations
whats_stable.content_item1: Blog — tenant content management and community news
whats_stable.content_item2: Resources and knowledge base — structured articles and shared resource library
whats_stable.content_item3: Help center — documentation hub and FAQ
whats_stable.content_item4: Newsletter system — email campaigns with smart segments, templates, and send-time optimisation
whats_stable.content_item5: AI Chat — AI-powered assistant for platform guidance
whats_stable.content_item7: Legal hub — versioned legal documents with acceptance gates and audit trail
whats_stable.platform_item1: Enterprise security — CSRF, rate limiting, TOTP 2FA, and WebAuthn passwordless auth
whats_stable.platform_item2: GDPR compliance — data requests, consent management, breach tracking, and audit log
whats_stable.platform_item3: Fraud and abuse detection — automated suspicious activity alerts and content moderation
whats_stable.platform_item5: 7 languages — English, Irish, German, French, Italian, Portuguese, and Spanish
whats_stable.platform_item6: Admin panel — tenant configuration, user management, analytics, and moderation
whats_stable.platform_item7: Algorithm controls — live health dashboard and tuning for all ranking systems

whats_rough.title: What may still be rough
whats_rough.item1: Federation (cross-community) features — functional but still accumulating real-world edge cases
whats_rough.item2: AI Chat assistant — depends on external AI providers; response quality varies by configuration
whats_rough.item3: Nexus Score breakdown — newly launched; scoring weights may be adjusted as usage data grows
whats_rough.item4: Smart feed ranking — EdgeRank algorithm is live but cold-start behaviour for new members is still being tuned
whats_rough.item5: Push notifications (mobile) — may have delivery delays on some devices or browsers
whats_rough.item6: Non-English translations — AI-generated and not yet reviewed by native speakers

how_we_catch_bugs.title: How we catch bugs
how_we_catch_bugs.intro: We use a multi-layer approach to catch and fix issues quickly:
how_we_catch_bugs.method1_text: 250+ PHP unit/integration tests and React component tests run on every commit
how_we_catch_bugs.method2_text: exceptions on both frontend and backend are captured in real time
how_we_catch_bugs.method3_text: traffic anomalies and error spikes are monitored continuously
how_we_catch_bugs.method4_text: core user journeys are tested across multiple tenant configurations before each deployment
how_we_catch_bugs.method5_text: real members across multiple active communities help us surface edge cases in production

how_to_help.title: How to help
how_to_help.report_bug_description: Found something broken? Please open an issue on GitHub — it's the fastest way to get a fix in front of us. Include what you were doing, what you expected, and what actually happened.
how_to_help.become_tester_description: If you run a timebank or community network and want to join the platform, we'd love to work with you. Reach out via GitHub Discussions or the contact page — we support communities of all sizes.

security.title: Security disclosure
security.description_before_email: If you discover a security vulnerability, please do not open a public issue. Instead, contact us confidentially at
security.description_after_email: . We will respond within 72 hours and work with you on responsible disclosure.
